content:
One of Angular’s Oldest Architectural Debates

3 min read
1 day ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

If you’ve worked with Angular for a while, you’ve probably heard this advice:
“Keep your components dumb.”
Or perhaps the opposite:
“Every feature needs a smart container component.”
The discussion has existed for years.
Yet when reviewing enterprise Angular applications, I rarely see teams applying these ideas consistently.
Not because they don’t understand the pattern.
Because many teams misunderstand its purpose.
The goal was never to create “smart” and “dumb” components.
The goal was to create clear responsibilities.
Not a Member? Read for FREE here.
The Problem Starts Small
Imagine a customer page.
Initially it looks like this:
CustomerComponentIt loads customers.
Filters them.
Handles search.
Updates selections.
